name: Desierto de Monegros – circular trail running to the Cartuja de Nuestra Señora de las Fuentes
name_original: Desierto de Monegros – circular de trail running a la Cartuja de Nuestra Señora de las Fuentes
region_intro: Jogging around Monegros offers a distinctive experience through a semi-desert region in Aragón, northeastern Spain. This unique environment provides a striking backdrop for runners, characterized by its arid landscapes, panoramic viewpoints, and geological formations known as "tozales." The region's varied terrain includes accessible paths and more challenging routes, with the Sierra de Alcubierre adding elevation and scenic vistas. After rainfall, the landscape can transform, offering a different visual experience for runners. Best jogging routes around Monegros The most popular jogging route is San Caprasio Circular Trail from Farlete, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 12 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views within the Sierra de Alcubierre. Another top favourite among local runners is Desierto de Monegros – circular trail running to the Cartuja de Nuestra Señora de las Fuentes, a moderate 3.2 miles (5.2 km) path. This route provides an opportunity to experience the semi-desert scenery while encountering a historical landmark. Local runners also love the Monegrillo Viewpoints Trail, a 12.6 miles (20.3 km) trail leading through unique semi-desert scenery and panoramic viewpoints, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes. Jogging around Monegros is defined by its semi-desert scenery, isolated "tozales" hills, and the varied terrain of the Sierra de Alcubierre. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Monegros are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from a few reviews. More than 50 runners have used komoot to explore Monegros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Monegros?
A: The Monegros region offers a diverse network of over 15 jogging routes, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find options ranging from easy paths to more challenging trails.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Monegros?
A: Yes, Monegros has several easy running routes perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Sariñena&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.7 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40638084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Grañén&lt;/a&gt; is another accessible option at 3.6 km.

Q: Can I find circular jogging routes in the Monegros region?
A: Many of the running routes in Monegros are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Notable circular trails include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983261477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Caprasio Circular Trail from Farlete&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949599758/desierto-de-monegros-circular-trail-running-to-the-cartuja-de-nuestra-senora-de-las-fuentes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desierto de Monegros – circular trail running to the Cartuja de Nuestra Señora de las Fuentes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of unique scenery can I expect while running in Monegros?
A: Monegros offers a distinctive semi-desert landscape, often described as having a 'wild west' feel. You'll encounter arid terrain, panoramic viewpoints, and unique geological formations known as 'tozales'. After rainfall, the landscape can transform into a vibrant green, offering a different visual experience.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the running trails?
A: Absolutely. The region combines natural beauty with historical elements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949599758/desierto-de-monegros-circular-trail-running-to-the-cartuja-de-nuestra-senora-de-las-fuentes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desierto de Monegros – circular trail&lt;/a&gt; passes by the historical Cartuja de Nuestra Señora de las Fuentes. In the Sierra de Alcubierre, you can explore historical caves, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Caprasio Caves&lt;/a&gt;, which are near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983261477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Caprasio Circular Trail from Farlete&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners say about their experience jogging in Monegros?
A: The running routes in Monegros are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Runners often praise the unique semi-desert scenery, the variety of trails, and the panoramic views, especially from routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983251798/monegrillo-viewpoints-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monegrillo Viewpoints Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running trails in Monegros suitable for families?
A: Monegros offers a range of trails, including easier, shorter loops that can be suitable for families. Towns like Grañén and Sariñena provide accessible options, often with paved surfaces, which might be more comfortable for family outings. Always check the route's distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Is Monegros a good place for dog-friendly running?
A: While the guide doesn't specifically list dog-friendly routes, many outdoor trails in Spain are generally accessible for dogs on a leash. Given the semi-arid environment, it's crucial to bring plenty of water for your dog, especially during warmer months, and be mindful of local regulations regarding pets in natural areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Monegros?
A: The best time for jogging in Monegros is typically during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot due to the semi-desert climate, making early mornings or late evenings the only viable times. Winters are generally cool and pleasant for running.

Q: Are there challenging running routes for experienced runners in Monegros?
A: Yes, Monegros provides challenging options for experienced runners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983261477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Caprasio Circular Trail from Farlete&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 17.1 km route with significant elevation gain, offering panoramic views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983251798/monegrillo-viewpoints-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monegrillo Viewpoints Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 20.3 km difficult trail.

Q: Are there running routes that offer panoramic views in Monegros?
A: Monegros is known for its panoramic views, especially in the Sierra de Alcubierre. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983261477" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Caprasio Circular Trail from Farlete&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983251798/monegrillo-viewpoints-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monegrillo Viewpoints Trail&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices for runners seeking expansive vistas of the unique semi-desert landscape. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bujal Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for stunning scenery.

Q: What towns offer good starting points for running loops in Monegros?
A: Several towns in Monegros serve as excellent starting points for running loops. Sariñena and Grañén are popular choices, offering accessible routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calle del Cine, Sariñena loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Grañén loop&lt;/a&gt;. Farlete and Monegrillo also provide access to more challenging trails.

Q: Are there any specific geological features to look out for on the trails?
A: Yes, a defining geological feature of Monegros is the presence of 'tozales' – unique isolated hills or mounds that dot the landscape. These formations contribute significantly to the region's distinctive appearance. You can find notable examples like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5771760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta&lt;/a&gt; and the general &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5228497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozales de Monegros&lt;/a&gt;.
tips:
The Charterhouse of Our Lady of the Fountains, also known as the Charterhouse of Monegros, is a former monastery located in the municipality of Sariñena, in the region of Monegros, province of Huesca, Aragon. Founded in 1507 by the Counts of Sástago, Beatriz de Luna, and Blasco de Aragón, it is the first Charterhouse built in the Kingdom of Aragon.

The current monastery, built in the 18th century, is an impressive architectural complex in the late Baroque style. It is notable for its mural paintings by Friar Manuel Bayeu, Goya's brother-in-law, depicting scenes from the life of the Virgin Mary, women of the Old Testament, and the cardinal virtues.

waypoints.name: Charterhouse of Our Lady of Las Fuentes
